segments.text: My breath is corrupt, my days are extinct, the graves are ready for me.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Are there not mockers with me? and doth not mine eye continue in their provocation?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Lay down now, put me in a surety with thee; who is he that will strike hands with me?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: For thou hast hid their heart from understanding: therefore shalt thou not exalt them.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He that speaketh flattery to his friends, even the eyes of his children shall fail.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: He hath made me also a byword of the people; and aforetime I was as a tabret.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Mine eye also is dim by reason of sorrow, and all my members are as a shadow.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: Upright men shall be astonied at this, and the innocent shall stir up himself against the hypocrite.
The righteous also shall hold on his way, and he that hath clean hands shall be stronger and stronger.
But as for you all, do ye return, and come now: for I cannot find one wise man among you.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: My days are past, my purposes are broken off, even the thoughts of my heart.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They change the night into day: the light is short because of darkness.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: If I wait, the grave is mine house: I have made my bed in the darkness.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: I have said to corruption, Thou art my father: to the worm, Thou art my mother, and my sister.
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: And where is now my hope? as for my hope, who shall see it?
segments.speaker: Job

segments.text: They shall go down to the bars of the pit, when our rest together is in the dust.
segments.speaker: Job
